layout.candidate_root_inheritance: all other PML4 entries exactly equal the retained PKMAP2 root

table_policy.table_frames: one eight-page contiguous DMA32 generation-owned PKPMM3 allocation
table_policy.data_frames: one contiguous following DMA32 generation-owned PKPMM3 page
table_policy.physical_address_width: live BSP CPUID leaf 0x80000008 constrained to 36..52 bits
table_policy.materialization: volatile writes through one PKMAP2 bootstrap temporary leaf with translation readback and per-transition INVLPG
table_policy.retained_mappings: kernel image, exact guarded stack, and immutable handoff inherited and independently walked before activation
table_policy.temporary_alias: shared inherited PKMAP2 leaf, revoked and translation-checked immediately before activation
table_policy.direct_map_permissions: supervisor writable NX write-back 4 KiB leaves for exactly eight table pages and one data page
table_policy.user_initial_permissions: user writable NX write-back 4 KiB leaf

transaction_policy.cr3_precondition: IF clear, BSP id 0, exact retained original CR3
transaction_policy.activation: write candidate CR3 and require exact readback
transaction_policy.activation_failure: write exact original CR3 and require exact rollback readback
transaction_policy.active_table_access: only through candidate direct-map virtual addresses
transaction_policy.architectural_accessed_dirty_bits: admitted as the only hardware-mutated leaf bits and preserved across protect
transaction_policy.protect_receipt: local INVLPG after exact user RW/NX to RX commit
transaction_policy.user_unmap_receipt: local INVLPG after exact user leaf revocation
transaction_policy.direct_unmap_receipt: local INVLPG after exact data direct-alias revocation
transaction_policy.frame_reuse: forbidden until user and direct unmap receipts bind root generation, BSP id, sequence, and virtual address
transaction_policy.restore: write exact original CR3 and require readback before table scrubbing
transaction_policy.release: scrub all eight inactive candidate table pages through the restored bootstrap mapping, revoke that mapping, then free tables

rejected_inputs: present temporary alias at activation boundary
unexpected PTE bits other than Accessed/Dirty
fault_controls: activation readback mismatch with exact rollback
active PTE write failure with exact rollback and no receipt
wrong root, CPU, and interrupt state rejection
trusted_for_this_profile: validated PKENTRY1 and PKMAP2 retained mappings
architectural MOV CR3 and INVLPG semantics

non_claims: PKVM2 activates and restores one candidate root on one qemu64 BSP with interrupts disabled; it does not start or coordinate application processors.
The direct map contains exactly eight candidate table pages and one data page; it is not a complete physical-memory direct map.
Three local INVLPG receipts are not an inter-processor shootdown protocol and do not prove SMP TLB coherence.
No ring 3 transition, syscall, PCID, global-page policy, huge page, COW, user-fault delivery, stack growth, pager, heap, reclaim, pressure, or OOM path is implemented.
The active profile is unsigned qemu64 development evidence and grants no authority or actions.
No target hardware, second host, N9 exit, release, or production claim is made.
